 Gold prices fall ahead of US inflation data, Fed decision. Here's what bullion dealers say - Livemint
(March 02 2026) Gold prices fell on Monday, March 2, 2026, ahead of the release of key US inflation data and a Federal Reserve decision. This decline comes as markets weigh prospects for interest rate adjustments and assess the impact of geopolitical developments in the Middle East. Bullion dealers reported subdued demand, with some noting a cautious sentiment among investors. The price of 24-carat gold in Delhi stood at ₹71,500 per 10 grams, down ₹200 from the previous close. The price of 22-carat gold was ₹65,550 per 10 grams, also down by ₹200. In Hyderabad, 24-carat gold was trading at ₹71,350 per 10 grams, a decrease of ₹250. The 22-carat gold in Hyderabad was ₹65,400 per 10 grams, down by ₹200. This is a developing story. This report will be updated as more information becomes available.
